# shelet

> SHELET — a 4-layer cognitive substrate (L0→L3) with cite-or-die discipline, designed for AI-in-the-loop systems where human ratification of held beliefs is irreducible. The slogan: 100% human choice. 100% machine execution.

## Core concepts

- [Thesis](README.md): the 4-lens model and the cite-or-die law
- [Chidushim index](chidushim/README.md): named held axioms with codification status
- [Chidush 019 — Substrate Conservation Law](chidushim/019-substrate-conservation-law.md): the conservation law that binds L0→L3 across substrate boundaries — L3 published outward becomes the next holder's L0
- [Chidush 021 — Agent Disclosure Protocol](chidushim/021-agent-disclosure-protocol.md): the policy that governs how AI agents acting on Mordechai's behalf must identify themselves in every outbound interaction

## The cognitive-sovereignty trinity

- Chidush 020 (input boundary): AI articulates; human ratifies what gets believed
- Chidush 016 (existence boundary): every actor in the substrate FKs to a sovereign human
- Chidush 021 (output boundary): every agent message identifies itself when speaking outward

## Implementation

- shelet-live (live substrate): https://github.com/mordechaipotash/shelet-live
- brain-mcp (one client): https://github.com/mordechaipotash/brain-mcp

## Author

- Mordechai Potash, AI engineer: https://mordechaipotash.com
- Beit Shemesh, Israel
